key_insight: EasyNano turns epitope-targeted nanobody CDR design into a fast gradient-based optimization problem by backpropagating through ESMFold2 distograms and a proximity-aware loss.
engineering_relevance: This enables practical, workstation-scale design of binders against user-chosen protein epitopes in minutes rather than days, making targeted biologics design more accessible.
method: Initialize CDR residue logits and optimize them with gradient descent
Use ESMFold2-Fast as a differentiable distogram oracle plus an epitope proximity loss
Add full ESMFold2 CA-coordinate priors to keep the nanobody framework pose stable
evidence_signal: Across six target-framework cases, EasyNano improved ipTM by up to 0.559, including Ty1/RBD from 0.143 to 0.702, and showed statistically significant gains over 30 random CDR baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends strongly on wild-type logit initialization and multi-seed runs, so local minima and run-to-run variability remain important practical concerns.
paper_title: EasyNano: rapid epitope-targeted nanobody CDR design via differentiable distogram optimization with ESMFold2
